subject: [VID] Use Helm to generate configuration files
commit_message:
[VID] Use Helm to generate configuration files

The property files were cooked inside the docker image and env variables assigned their values
with the help of 'sed' commands - manual pattern-check and replacements. This (apart from the
obvious reason to use proper configmap type mapping and let Helm find out the values for you)
sed command based replacement that caused many issues with special characters when VID
application was started and the env variabels (having those special characters) were attempted
by sed to be repalced by their values.

Instead of doing all this 'magic' using sed, we now used the configmaps to map volumes containing
our property files which are using those environment variabels as they are - no need to replace anything
since helm/k8s will expose all those env variables to you. There is not need to play with the files
content by manually replacing one value with the other.

Also, the vid/value.yaml file has been updated to use the new image (7.0.2) which doesn't do these sed
commands).

Issue-ID: VID-890
Change-Id: Ie9f0b31c259399d2f877e991a9110dfdc1f32389
Signed-off-by: Ikramullah, Ikram <ikram@research.att.com>

Patch Set 1:

the commit message (on OOM) must follow this form:

[NAME_OF_COMPONENT|DOC|COMMON|GENERIC] Meaningful title (from OOM side)

at least one sentence explaining the change done in this patch, cause and consequences and possibly more of course

Issue-ID: AS_WE_ARE_FORCED_BUT_MEANINGLESS
Change-ID: xxx
Sign-off: xxx

Commit message will be the last stuff that will stay with our code so it must clearly explain the changes, the "why" and the consequences. If it change OOM behavior in any way, documentation must be also updated.

Starting Frankfurt branching, merge requests which are not following this pattern will not be merged.

Please read the following pages and follow the guidelines for writing commit message contained therein.

    http://bit.ly/goodcommitmessages
    http://who-t.blogspot.com/2009/12/on-commit-messages.html
    http://dep.debian.net/deps/dep3/
